title: Cutii de pantofi, legionari, motorină și altele
summary: A satirical commentary by Cristian Preda cataloging the absurdities of Romanian politics in a single week, including a minister's former chief of staff hiding 500,000 euros in shoe boxes, parliamentary attempts to decriminalize praise of war criminals, chaotic fuel price compensation decisions, and legislative flip-flopping. The author uses rhetorical questions to highlight how uniquely dysfunctional Romania's political scene is.
talking_points: A former ministerial chief of staff was caught storing 500,000 euros in shoe boxes and was recorded by prosecutors whispering obscenities

An opposition parliamentary group submitted a bill to decriminalize the glorification of war criminals (Legionnaires), while a court lifted judicial control on someone investigated for praising political assassins
The government spent three weeks discussing fuel price compensation only to produce an absurd outcome where premium diesel ended up cheaper than standard
Parliament admitted, rejected, and then re-admitted the same law within one hour, showcasing legislative chaos
The author questions why early elections are treated as more dangerous than war itself, criticizing the political establishment's resistance to democratic crisis resolution
author: Cristian Preda
